senses.definition: to barely manage to survive or succeed

examples.x: After losing his job, John was hanging on by his fingernails until he found a new one.
The company was hanging on by its fingernails during the recession, but it managed to survive thanks to some smart cost-cutting measures.
The team was hanging on by their fingernails in the final minutes of the game, but they managed to hold on for the win.

senses.definition: in a difficult, desperate, or vulnerable situation where help or intervention may be needed

examples.x: After the scandal broke, the politician was on his knees begging for forgiveness from the public.
The company was on its knees after the market crash, struggling to stay afloat.

senses.definition: to proceed in a way that causes a bad outcome

examples.x: I was trying to fix the leaky faucet, but then everything went pear-shaped and I ended up flooding the bathroom.
The company's plan to expand its business in Europe went pear-shaped when the economy took a downturn.

word_list.pronunciation: fˈɔːl æt ðə fˈɜːst hˈɜːdəl fˈɛns

senses.definition: to fail or encounter difficulties at the beginning of a task or process, before any significant progress has been made

examples.x: I was hoping to learn how to play the guitar, but I fell at the first fence when I realized how difficult it was to even hold the instrument correctly.
The athlete fell at the first hurdle of the race, ending his chances of winning a medal.
The new restaurant fell at the first hurdle when it received a terrible review in the local newspaper, causing a sharp drop in business.
The student fell at the first fence of the exam by spending too much time on the first question and not leaving enough time for the rest.
The team's plan to launch a new product fell at the first hurdle when they realized they didn't have the necessary funding to bring it to market.

word_list.pronunciation: fˈɔːl æt ðə fˈaɪnəl ɔːɹ lˈæst hˈɜːdəl ɔːɹ fˈɛns

senses.definition: to encounter failure or difficulties just before reaching the end or achieving a goal

examples.x: Despite an impressive job interview, he unfortunately fell at the final fence when he failed to pass the required skills test.
The candidate was leading in the polls throughout the campaign, but a major scandal emerged just before the election caused him to fall at the final hurdle.
After months of negotiation, the business deal seemed promising, but it fell at the last fence when unexpected terms were introduced.
The student excelled in all subjects throughout the semester, only to fall at the last hurdle by failing the final exam.
The team had a great season, but unfortunately, they fell at the last hurdle and didn't make it to the championship.
After months of training, the athlete fell at the final hurdle during the championship race.

word_list.pronunciation: ðə wˈiːlz fˈɔːl ˈɔf kˈʌm ˈɔf

senses.definition: used to refer to something that was working or progressing well but suddenly stopped functioning or fell apart

examples.x: The company's financial situation was looking stable, but when a major investor pulled out, the wheels came off and the company went bankrupt.
The team was leading the game, but when their star player was injured, the wheels fell off and they lost by a large margin.

senses.definition: (of a joke, remark, event, etc.) to be completely unsuccessful in amusing people or having the desired effect

examples.x: The comedian's joke fell flat and no one in the audience laughed.
The new product launch fell flat when it failed to generate much interest or sales.
The team's attempt at a trick play fell flat and resulted in a turnover.
Despite his enthusiasm, the sales pitch fell flat with the potential clients.
The event’s opening act fell flat, failing to energize the crowd for the main performance.

senses.definition: to experience a complete failure or defeat, often accompanied by dramatic or disastrous consequences

examples.x: The company's ambitious expansion plans went up in flames when their major investor pulled out.
The project is going up in flames due to mismanagement.

senses.definition: to experience an embarrassing failure in an attempt to do something

examples.x: The actor thought he could improvise his lines, but he fell flat on his face and forgot what to say.
The athlete thought he could easily clear the high jump, but he fell flat on his face and didn't even make it over the bar.
The speaker had not prepared for the presentation and so when she got up to speak, she fell flat on her face and forgot her lines.
The student thought he could pass the exam without studying, but he fell flat on his face and failed.

senses.definition: to suddenly fall down because of losing one's balance

examples.x: The clumsy waiter was carrying a tray of drinks when he tripped and went ass over tip, spilling the drinks all over the customers.
The hiker slipped on a loose rock and went ass over tip down the steep slope.
The skateboarder tried to do a trick, but he lost his balance and went ass over tit.
The toddler was running too fast and tripped, going ass over tit on the playground.

senses.definition: to experience a sudden and significant decline, deterioration, or devaluation, often unexpectedly or rapidly

examples.x: The company's financial situation was stable, but when a major client cancelled their contract, things went south quickly.
The project was going well, but when the lead developer left, everything went south.
The weather was beautiful all morning, but in the afternoon, it suddenly went south and it started raining heavily.

senses.definition: to malfunction or stop functioning properly

examples.x: The car's engine started making strange noises, and then the dashboard lights went haywire and started flashing.
The computer system went haywire and started deleting important files.
The experiment seemed to be going well, but then the equipment malfunctioned and things went haywire.

word_list.pronunciation: bˈiːt ɔːɹ bˈæŋ ɔːɹ hˈɪt wˈʌnz hˈɛd ɐɡˈɛnst ɐ ɔːɹ ðə bɹˈɪk ɔːɹ wˈɔːl

senses.definition: to repeatedly try to do or achieve something without success

examples.x: After numerous attempts to convince the stubborn team member to cooperate, it felt like I was banging my head against a brick wall.
Despite countless meetings and presentations, the project manager felt as if he were hitting his head against a brick wall when trying to implement changes in the company's workflow.
The environmental activist expressed frustration, feeling like she was banging her head against a wall, as the calls for sustainable practices went unanswered by the industry leaders.
The teacher felt like she was hitting her head against a wall while trying to get the students to complete their assignments on time.
The inventor found himself beating his head against a brick wall, trying to convince skeptical investors of the revolutionary potential of his new technology.
After several unsuccessful job applications, he felt like he was beating his head against a wall in his quest for employment.

word_list.pronunciation: ˌɛsbˈiː kˈænt ɔːɹ kˌʊdənt hˈæk ɪt

senses.definition: used to convey that one is unable to cope with or handle a particular situation, task, or challenge
(of a company, organization, plan, etc.) to experience a complete failure

examples.x: The bank had invested heavily in risky loans, which eventually caused it to go belly up.
The company had been in business for decades, but when it couldn't keep up with changing market trends, it went belly up.
The restaurant was struggling to attract customers, and eventually went belly up.
The startup had high hopes, but when it failed to secure funding, it went belly up.

senses.definition: an issue causing progress to slow down or stop entirely

examples.x: The unexpected budget cuts dealt a black eye to our project's progress.
The economic downturn has been a black eye for the country's financial stability.

senses.definition: to completely fail to be effective

examples.x: The actor forgot his lines during the performance, causing the scene to fall to pieces and the audience to become confused.
The company's financial situation was already precarious, and then the loss of a major client caused everything to fall to pieces.
The old car was already starting to fall to pieces, and then it finally broke down on the side of the road.
The team had been struggling all season, and when their star player was injured, their performance fell to pieces.
